title: Sync both referenced tag and `--append-suffix` tag to registry
body:
I am currently utilising Skopeo to sync a number of images from various upstream registries to a private registry and would like to now start to use the `--append-suffix` flag. However, I still need to sync the referenced tag for each image. 

For example, if I want to sync the image `docker.io/amazon/aws-cli:2.25.10` to my registry and set `--append-suffix "-upstream"` I would like to see tags `2.25.10` and `2.25.10-upstream` in my registry. Currently when using the `--apend-suffix` flag I only see the tag with the suffix.

Is there a way to achieve the above without running the `skopeo sync` command twice - once without the `--append-suffix` flag and once with.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

That’s not an available feature — but, also, there’s really not that much of a benefit to adding it. As currently implemented, at least, each of the tag copy/sync works mostly stand-alone, and the only thing that could be saved by doing both in a single run is the tag list registry operations.

So I don’t think that adding an extra option that is ~no better than running the command twice would be a significant enough improvement to be worth the extra complexity. (That might change in some hypothetical future, if _much more_ state were shared across the individual tag/image copies. But we are rather far from that.)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
